activities_projects.title: Graphical Analysis of Motion
activities_projects.objective: Plot and interpret distance-time and speed-time graphs
steps: Measure distance and time at small intervals for moving object
Record data accurately
Plot distance vs time and speed vs time graphs
Analyze graphs to determine uniformity and changes in speed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands graphical representation and analysis of motion
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct plotting and interpretation of graphs (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Effect of Inclination on Motion
activities_projects.objective: Study how slope angle affects speed of an object
steps: Set ramp at different angles
Measure distance along ramp
Time the object moving down ramp for each angle
Calculate speed and compare results
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the relationship between slope and speed
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate measurement, calculation, and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparing Motion on Different Surfaces with Inclines
activities_projects.objective: Investigate effect of friction and slope on motion
steps: Set ramp on different surface materials
Time object moving down each surface
Calculate speed and compare across surfaces
Analyze how friction affects motion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands combined effect of slope and friction on speed

activities_projects.title: Investigating Period of Pendulum with Multiple Lengths
activities_projects.objective: Study how pendulum length affects time period precisely
steps: Set pendulum with different lengths
Measure time for 20 complete swings for each length
Calculate average time period
Analyze relationship between length and time period
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate measurements, calculations, and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Instantaneous Speed Using Short Intervals
activities_projects.objective: Calculate instantaneous speed over very small distances
steps: Mark very short distances along the track
Record time taken for each short segment
Calculate speed for each interval
Compare instantaneous speed with average speed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands concept and calculation of instantaneous speed

activities_projects.title: Reaction Time Analysis for Multiple People
activities_projects.objective: Compare reaction times across multiple individuals
steps: Perform ruler drop test for at least 5 individuals
Record distances for each trial
Calculate reaction time for each person
Analyze variations and calculate average reaction time
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes human reaction time variability statistically

activities_projects.title: Motion with Varying Mass
activities_projects.objective: Study how mass affects speed on a slope
steps: Place different weights on toy car
Measure time to travel down slope for each weight
Calculate speed and compare results
Analyze how mass influences motion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands effect of mass on motion quantitatively
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate measurement, calculation, and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparing Average Speeds of Multiple Objects
activities_projects.objective: Analyze relative motion using multiple objects
steps: Time different objects moving same distance
Calculate average speed for each object
Compare results and explain differences
Discuss reasons for variations in speed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands relative speed and factors affecting motion
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate calculation, comparison, and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Graphical Comparison of Speeds
activities_projects.objective: Use graphs to analyze multiple motion trials
steps: Record speed data for multiple trials of an object
Plot speed-time graphs for each trial
Compare graphs to analyze motion consistency
Identify patterns or anomalies
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical skills using graphical data
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct graphing, interpretation, and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Design a Complex Motion Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Apply concepts creatively in a multi-variable motion study
steps: Design experiment involving at least two variables (mass, slope, surface type)
Predict results based on theory
Conduct experiment and record measurements
Analyze results and compare with predictions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Encourages creativity, multi-variable analysis, and scientific reasoning
